graphEvents.label: fallback token count explains 2 percent symptom

question: Q5
title: The 2 percent budget signal is post-truncation telemetry
summary: The handler historically reported fallbackTokens as enforcement.actualTokens after replacing the payload with a tiny empty envelope, so probes saw low budget utilization after truncation rather than the original overflow size.
recommendation: Expose separate preEnforcementTokens and returnedTokens fields; keep actualTokens backward-compatible only if clearly documented.

question: Q5
title: Historical fallback ladder zero-filled before preserving survivors
summary: The pre-fix fallback path tried only count:0/results:[] inner envelopes and could leave returnedResultCount from earlier truncation stages, creating metadata/payload disagreement.
recommendation: Never fall back to empty results while any survivor can fit as metadata-only; derive returnedResultCount from the emitted nested payload.

question: Q5
title: Direct memory_search bypass confirms underlying retrieval is intact
summary: 006 live probes show memory_search returned relevant CocoIndex hits while memory_context zero-filled, isolating the fault to the L1 wrapper/enforcer layer.

question: Q5
title: Outer context-server budget pass is not the nested zero-fill origin
summary: context-server.ts trims envelope.data.results directly, while memory_context stores search hits inside envelope.data.content[0].text; this pass can add metadata or warnings but does not explain the nested count:0 payload.
